title: Identify Available Buttons On The Hospital HMI Maintenance Menu
summary: Use the Hospital HMI Maintenance Menu screen to identify the four available buttons and confirm which information areas are accessible from that menu.
when_to_use: Use when verifying the contents of the Hospital HMI Maintenance Menu screen or confirming which information areas are available from that menu.
not_for: Do not use this runbook to infer the function or behavior of any button beyond the source-stated access to information.
Do not use this runbook as a procedure for operating the Manual Control, IO Diagnostics, Persistent Variables, or Default screens.

roles_and_responsibilities.responsibility: View the Hospital HMI Maintenance Menu and verify that the documented four buttons are present.

safety_notes: This runbook is limited to identification and verification of menu entries only.
Do not infer the function or behavior of any button beyond the source-stated access to information.
access_or_tools_needed: Access to the Hospital HMI Maintenance Menu screen
Figure 4-38 or equivalent documented Maintenance Menu reference

access_required: Access to view the Hospital HMI Maintenance Menu screen
system_preconditions: The Hospital HMI Maintenance Menu screen is available for viewing.
external_references: Figure 4-38, Hospital HMI Maintenance Menu Screen

visual_references.description: Primary reference image showing the Hospital HMI Maintenance Menu and its four buttons.
Related Hospital HMI main menu reference that may help orient navigation to the Maintenance Menu.
Related reference for the IO Diagnostics destination screen named on the Maintenance Menu.
Related reference for the Default destination screen named on the Maintenance Menu.

steps.title: Open or view the Maintenance Menu screen
steps.purpose: Access the Hospital HMI screen that contains the documented maintenance menu entries.
steps.instruction: Open or view the Hospital HMI Maintenance Menu screen.
steps.expected_result: The Hospital HMI Maintenance Menu screen is visible for review.
steps.healthy_condition: A Hospital HMI screen matching the Maintenance Menu layout is displayed.
steps.failure_condition: The Maintenance Menu screen cannot be viewed or the visible screen does not match the documented Maintenance Menu reference.

actions.label: View Maintenance Menu screen
actions.exact_action: Open or view the Hospital HMI Maintenance Menu screen.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Overall Maintenance Menu screen layout and available maintenance access buttons.

stop_or_escalate_if: The Maintenance Menu screen cannot be accessed for viewing.
The visible screen does not appear to be the documented Maintenance Menu.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: Figure 4-38, titled "Hospital HMI Maintenance Menu Screen," appears on page 69 of the OptiSweep Operation and Maintenance Manual in section 4.1.3.3, Maintenance Menu.

steps.title: Confirm the screen title and menu identity
steps.purpose: Verify that the displayed screen is specifically the documented Maintenance Menu.
steps.instruction: Confirm the screen is the "Maintenance Menu."
steps.expected_result: The displayed screen is identified as the Maintenance Menu.
steps.healthy_condition: The screen matches Figure 4-38 and is recognized as the Hospital HMI Maintenance Menu.
steps.failure_condition: The screen title or layout does not match the documented Maintenance Menu reference.

actions.label: Confirm Maintenance Menu identity
actions.exact_action: Confirm the screen is the "Maintenance Menu."

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Screen title and overall layout corresponding to Figure 4-38.

stop_or_escalate_if: The displayed screen cannot be confirmed as the Maintenance Menu.
The screen layout differs from the documented Maintenance Menu reference.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: The "Maintenance Menu" has four buttons that allow access to a variety of information: 2.1 Manual Control, 2.2 IO Diagnostics, 2.3 Persistent Variables, 2.4 Default.

steps.title: Locate the four documented menu buttons
steps.purpose: Identify each button that the source states is available from the Maintenance Menu.
steps.instruction: Locate the four buttons shown on the menu: "2.1 Manual Control," "2.2 IO Diagnostics," "2.3 Persistent Variables," and "2.4 Default."
steps.expected_result: All four documented buttons are visible on the Maintenance Menu.
steps.healthy_condition: The menu shows exactly the four documented entries: 2.1 Manual Control, 2.2 IO Diagnostics, 2.3 Persistent Variables, and 2.4 Default.
steps.failure_condition: One or more of the documented buttons are missing, renamed, or not visible.

actions.label: Identify four menu buttons
actions.exact_action: Locate the four buttons shown on the menu: "2.1 Manual Control," "2.2 IO Diagnostics," "2.3 Persistent Variables," and "2.4 Default."

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: The four Maintenance Menu buttons: 2.1 Manual Control, 2.2 IO Diagnostics, 2.3 Persistent Variables, and 2.4 Default.

stop_or_escalate_if: The visible Maintenance Menu does not show the four documented buttons.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: The "Maintenance Menu" has four buttons that allow access to a variety of information: 2.1 Manual Control, 2.2 IO Diagnostics, 2.3 Persistent Variables, 2.4 Default.

steps.title: Compare the visible menu to the documented list
steps.purpose: Verify that the displayed Maintenance Menu matches the source documentation.
steps.instruction: Compare the visible buttons on the screen to the documented list of four Maintenance Menu entries.
steps.expected_result: The visible menu entries match the documented list of four buttons.
steps.healthy_condition: The displayed buttons align with the documented Figure 4-38 list without discrepancy.
steps.failure_condition: The displayed buttons do not match the documented list of four Maintenance Menu entries.

actions.label: Compare menu entries to documentation
actions.exact_action: Compare the visible buttons on the screen to the documented list of four Maintenance Menu entries.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Documented Maintenance Menu entries for one-to-one comparison with the live or viewed screen.

stop_or_escalate_if: The visible Maintenance Menu does not show the four documented buttons.
Any discrepancy exists between the displayed menu and the documented list.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: The "Maintenance Menu" has four buttons that allow access to a variety of information: 2.1 Manual Control, 2.2 IO Diagnostics, 2.3 Persistent Variables, 2.4 Default.

steps.title: Record the available information areas
steps.purpose: Document which information areas are available from the Maintenance Menu based on the source-supported entries.
steps.instruction: Record which of the documented information areas are available from the Maintenance Menu.
steps.expected_result: The available information areas are recorded as Manual Control, IO Diagnostics, Persistent Variables, and Default.
steps.healthy_condition: The recorded list matches the four documented Maintenance Menu entries.
steps.failure_condition: The recorded list is incomplete, inconsistent with the visible menu, or inconsistent with the documented source.

actions.label: Record available information areas
actions.exact_action: Record which of the documented information areas are available from the Maintenance Menu.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: The four documented Maintenance Menu entries to record as available information areas.

stop_or_escalate_if: The visible Maintenance Menu does not show the four documented buttons.
The available information areas cannot be recorded exactly as documented.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: The "Maintenance Menu" has four buttons that allow access to a variety of information: 2.1 Manual Control, 2.2 IO Diagnostics, 2.3 Persistent Variables, 2.4 Default.

success_criteria: The Hospital HMI Maintenance Menu is identified correctly.
The four documented buttons are confirmed as visible: 2.1 Manual Control, 2.2 IO Diagnostics, 2.3 Persistent Variables, and 2.4 Default.
The available information areas are recorded exactly as documented by the source.
healthy_conditions: The displayed screen matches the documented Hospital HMI Maintenance Menu reference.
All four documented Maintenance Menu entries are present and readable.
The recorded available information areas match the source documentation.
failure_conditions: The Maintenance Menu screen cannot be confirmed.
One or more of the four documented buttons are missing or do not match the source.
The user attempts to infer button behavior beyond the source-supported description.
escalation_guidance: Escalate if the visible Maintenance Menu does not show the four documented buttons.
Escalate if the displayed screen cannot be matched to Figure 4-38.
Do not infer the function or behavior of any button beyond the source-stated access to information.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Primary source image for the Maintenance Menu title, layout, and four documented buttons.

source_refs.quote_or_summary: The "Maintenance Menu" has four buttons that allow access to a variety of information: 2.1 Manual Control, 2.2 IO Diagnostics, 2.3 Persistent Variables, 2.4 Default.

missing_details: The source does not provide a time estimate for this verification.
The source does not specify exact navigation steps to open the Maintenance Menu from another screen within this section.
The source does not describe the detailed behavior of the Manual Control, IO Diagnostics, Persistent Variables, or Default buttons within the Maintenance Menu section.
The source does not specify whether production stop or LOTO is required for this reference activity.
